content: TreeQA uses logic-tree reasoning to verify intermediate steps before producing a final answer.
Hybrid retrieval combines vector evidence with graph-backed factual support to improve coverage.
Validation can use an LLM judge or lexical overlap heuristics as a factuality signal before final answer generation.
Query decomposition breaks a complex multi-hop question into smaller verifiable sub-questions.
TreeQA's corrector agent rewrites a query when a sub-answer fails validation to attempt a better retrieval pass.
The pipeline retries a leaf node up to max_retries times when the validator confidence is below the threshold.
HotpotQA is a multi-hop QA dataset with over 113,000 question-answer pairs drawn from two Wikipedia paragraphs.
2WikiMultiHopQA requires reasoning across up to four evidence hops and annotates explicit reasoning chains.
Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR) uses bi-encoder models to embed queries and passages into a shared vector space.
BM25 is a sparse retrieval method based on term frequency and inverse document frequency.
Hybrid retrieval fuses BM25 lexical scores with dense embedding scores, typically outperforming either alone.
RAGAS measures faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision, and context recall for RAG pipelines.
LLM hallucination occurs when the model generates text not supported by retrieved evidence or contradicts the source.
LangGraph represents agent workflows as directed graphs where nodes are computation steps and edges define control flow.
LangGraph checkpointing saves state at each node boundary, enabling pausing, resuming, and replaying workflows.
Self-RAG trains the language model to decide when to retrieve and to critique its own outputs against evidence.
ReAct is an agent pattern that alternates between reasoning and acting in a loop until the task is complete.
OpenRouter provides a unified OpenAI-compatible API gateway to many open and commercial language models.
Local vector index in TreeQA stores chunked document text in JSONL format for lexical retrieval without a vector database.
MuSiQue enforces that each question requires all hops by verifying that partial paths cannot answer the question alone.
